title: heliconia longiflora subsp. ecuadoriensis
intro: Heliconia longiflora subsp. ecuadoriensis, also known as Ecuadorian heliconia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Heliconiaceae, characterized by its long, waxy flowers and shrub-like appearance. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Heliconia longiflora subsp. ecuadoriensis include relief from coughs and congestion, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce inflammation. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are made by steeping the flowers, leaves, and roots in hot water or a solvent.
